violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, soup was placed in buckets greater than four inches deep in the walk-in cooler with a lid. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025
Tongs were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen and bar was lacking proper handwashing sign. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as bamboo shoots) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC removed eggs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took ice machine out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Noodles and tempura batter were observed held between 44-63 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved items to walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Various items observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Rodent bait was observed uncovered in the basement area. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, soup was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC placed soup in shallower containers for cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Thermometer was observed in the 3 door lowboy in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Working containers of ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC informed to end practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Old seaweed containers were observed used to store other food items in the walk-in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Deli slicer located in the kitchen area was not in good repair (taped handles) New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Plastic wrap and foil was observed lining the shelves in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
3 door lowboy located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. PIC called for service during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-deep freezer chests,-reach-in freezers,-prep units,-prep tables,-lowboy coolers,-deli slicer,-funnel,-blender base,-food processor,-mandoline. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2025

A heavy accumulation of grease was observed in the basement near the grease trap. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
